name: Maya
regret: I didn't call my grandmother back the week she died. I told myself I'd call Sunday. She died Saturday morning and I still can't forgive the version of me who thought there'd be time.
proud: The week my little brother started therapy. I had begged him for two years. When he finally sent me the text saying 'okay, I did it' I sat in my car for an hour and cried.
dream: I keep starting a letter to my father. I've written the first line maybe fifty times. I don't know if I want to send it or just finish writing it.

name: Daniel
regret: I quit music when I was nineteen because someone I respected said I wasn't good enough to make it. I was. I just believed him instead of myself.
proud: I taught myself to cook the recipes my mom made before she got sick. The first time I made her khao soi for my daughter and she said 'this tastes like grandma' I knew I'd gotten something back.
dream: To write down the stories my mother never told me. I have fragments. I want to put them in order before I forget.

name: Priya
regret: I stayed in a job that was killing something in me for four years because I was afraid of starting over. I didn't realize until I left how much of myself I had packed into a drawer.
proud: I walked away from a relationship that looked good on paper but was quietly shrinking me. No one in my family understood. I still don't regret it.
dream: I want to open a small bookshop that only sells books I've actually read. I keep making the spreadsheet and closing it.

name: Jordan
regret: I wasn't there the night my best friend called me. I saw the missed call the next morning. He's okay now, but I know I wasn't the one who picked up.
proud: I finished my master's program after my dad stopped believing I would. He came to the ceremony anyway. We didn't talk about it but I saw him cry during the procession.
dream: To learn to be the kind of friend I was supposed to be that night. I don't know what that looks like yet but I'm trying.

name: Elena
regret: I told my mother I forgave her before I actually did. I said it because I wanted the conversation to be over. I've been quietly un-forgiving her ever since.
proud: The morning I realized I had become the person my twelve-year-old self was waiting for. I was doing the dishes. No one saw it. I was crying into the sink.
dream: To write the thing I've been not writing for seven years. I know what it's about. I'm scared of what it'll say when it's finished.
